title: Add helm-validate CI job with lint, template scenarios, and unittest
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** GitHub Issue [#121](https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/121)

Add a `helm-validate` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` gated by a new `helm` path filter (`operators/keystone/helm/**`) in the existing `dorny/paths-filter` config. The job: (1) sets up Helm via `azure/setup-helm`, (2) installs helm-unittest plugin with pinned version via `helm plugin install`, (3) runs `helm lint` on the keystone-operator chart, (4) runs `helm template` with 4 value override scenarios (defaults, webhook.enabled=false, serviceAccount.create=false+serviceAccount.name=existing-sa, minimal resource overrides), (5) runs `helm unittest`. Create unittest files under `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/` for deployment (image, replicas, resources, securityContext, probes, leader-elect arg, webhook volume mount), clusterrole (RBAC rules coverage), clusterrolebinding (role ref + subject), service (metrics port, conditional webhook port), and webhook-configuration (present when enabled, absent when disabled).

**Rationale:** The CI pipeline validates Go code but has zero Helm chart validation. Template errors, broken value wiring, and invalid YAML are only caught at deploy time. This has caused silent breakage when chart values were renamed or conditionals were misconfigured. Adding lint+template+unittest catches these issues at PR time and prevents broken releases.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/ci.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/deployment_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/clusterrole_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/clusterrolebinding_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/service_test.yaml
- operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/tests/webhook_test.yaml
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Feature ID:** CC-0121

---

## Open Questions

All questions resolved by user — no remaining ambiguities.

- **Helm-unittest runner:** Manual plugin install via `helm plugin install` (user-selected). Version-pinned in CI, full control over caching.
- **Value override scenarios:** Defaults + webhook disabled + existing serviceAccount + minimal resources (4 scenarios covering the main conditional branches).

---

## Summary

Add a dedicated `helm-validate` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` that runs `helm lint`, `helm template` with four value override scenarios, and `helm-unittest` against the `keystone-operator` chart. A new `helm` path filter (following the existing `dorny/paths-filter` pattern) gates the job on changes under `operators/keystone/helm/**`. The existing `make verify-crd-sync` step in the `verify-codegen` job already satisfies the CRD sync acceptance criterion — no changes needed there.

---

## Scope

**Included:**
- New path filter `helm` in the `changes` job matching `operators/keystone/helm/**`
- New CI job `helm-validate` in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` running `helm lint`, `helm template` (4 scenarios), and `helm-unittest`
- Manual `helm plugin install https://github.com/helm-unittest/helm-unittest` step with pinned version
- New `tests/` directory under `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/` with unittest YAML files for deployment, clusterrole, clusterrolebinding, service, and webhook-configuration templates
- Four `helm template` scenarios: defaults, `webhook.enabled=false`, `serviceAccount.create=false` + `serviceAccount.name=existing-sa`, minimal resource overrides

**Excluded:**
- CRD sync verification changes — already present in `verify-codegen` job (`make verify-crd-sync` step)
- Helm chart functional changes — this is CI/test infrastructure only
- Tests for `_helpers.tpl`, `certificate.yaml`, `serviceaccount.yaml` — low-risk boilerplate templates (YAGNI; add later if breakage occurs)
- Makefile targets for local helm testing — CI-only scope per the issue
- Changes to `helm-push` or release jobs
- `leaderElection.enabled` as a separate `helm template` scenario — covered by deployment unittest assertions on `--leader-elect` arg

---

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    PR["Push / PR"] --> CHG["changes job (paths-filter)"]
    CHG -->|"operators/keystone/helm/**"| HV["helm-validate job"]
    CHG -->|"operators/keystone/**"| VC["verify-codegen job (existing)"]

    subgraph HV["helm-validate"]
        SETUP["Setup Helm + plugin install"] --> LINT["helm lint"]
        LINT --> TPL["helm template (4 scenarios)"]
        TPL --> UT["helm unittest"]
    end

    subgraph VC["verify-codegen (existing)"]
        MAN["make manifests"] --> GEN["make generate"]
        GEN --> SYNC["make verify-crd-sync"]
        SYNC --> DIFF["git diff --exit-code"]
    end

    HV -->|"fail = block merge"| RESULT["CI Status"]
    VC -->|"fail = block merge"| RESULT
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph unittest["helm-unittest test files"]
        DEP["deployment_test.yaml"]
        CR["clusterrole_test.yaml"]
        CRB["clusterrolebinding_test.yaml"]
        SVC["service_test.yaml"]
        WH["webhook_test.yaml"]
    end

    DEP -->|asserts| DTPL["deployment.yaml"]
    CR -->|asserts| CRTPL["clusterrole.yaml"]
    CRB -->|asserts| CRBTPL["clusterrolebinding.yaml"]
    SVC -->|asserts| STPL["service.yaml"]
    WH -->|asserts| WTPL["webhook-configuration.yaml"]
```

---

## Key Components

- **Path filter `helm`**: New entry in `changes` job's `dorny/paths-filter` config matching `operators/keystone/helm/**`, following the existing pattern (`go_common`, `keystone`, `docs`, `e2e_infra`).
- **CI job `helm-validate`**: New job in `ci.yaml` conditional on `needs.changes.outputs.helm == 'true'`. Sets up Helm via `azure/setup-helm`, installs helm-unittest plugin manually with version pin, then runs lint → template → unittest sequentially.
- **`helm lint` step**: Validates chart structure, `Chart.yaml`, and template syntax for `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/`.
- **`helm template` step**: Renders templates with 4 value override scenarios and fails on render errors:
  1. Default values (no overrides)
  2. `--set webhook.enabled=false`
  3. `--set serviceAccount.create=false --set serviceAccount.name=existing-sa`
  4. `--set resources.limits.cpu=100m --set resources.limits.memory=64Mi`
- **`helm-unittest` step**: Runs `helm unittest operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/` against test files in the chart's `tests/` directory.
- **`deployment_test.yaml`**: Asserts container image, replicas, resource limits, security context (runAsNonRoot, user 65532), probe paths (`/healthz`, `/readyz` on port 8081), `--leader-elect` and `--metrics-bind-address` args, conditional webhook volume mount.
- **`clusterrole_test.yaml`**: Asserts expected RBAC rules cover keystones, deployments, jobs, services, secrets, mariadb resources, externalsecrets, networkpolicies, HPAs, PDBs.
- **`clusterrolebinding_test.yaml`**: Asserts role binding references the correct ClusterRole and ServiceAccount subject.
- **`service_test.yaml`**: Asserts port configuration — metrics port from `.Values.metrics.port` (8080), conditional webhook port (443→9443 when `webhook.enabled=true`).
- **`webhook_test.yaml`**: Asserts MutatingWebhookConfiguration and ValidatingWebhookConfiguration render when `webhook.enabled=true` and are absent when `false`.
- **CRD sync verification**: Already confirmed in CI at `verify-codegen` job — no new work required.
